macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Drives Oil Above $90, Gas Prices Hit $4 as Markets Brace for Inflation
items.summary: Oil prices jumped 3% on Monday with Brent surpassing $90 a barrel amid escalating U.S.-Iran attacks curbing shipments in the Strait of Hormuz, pushing U.S. gas prices back to $4 a gallon and stoking inflation fears ahead of earnings season [1][7].

items.title: Stock Futures Rise Slightly After Chip Pullback; Investors Await Key Tech Earnings
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures rose slightly on Monday following last week's chip-stocks-led pullback, as investors await major tech earnings from Alphabet and Tesla this week that could test Wall Street's AI-driven rally [1][11].

items.title: European and Asian Shares Hesitant on Oil Spike and Middle East Tensions
items.summary: European shares edged down and Asian markets were hesitant on Monday as the escalating Gulf conflict lifted oil prices and fanned inflation fears, while a packed week of tech earnings tests investor faith in the AI trade [1].

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company at $4.88 Trillion
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot as the world's most valuable company with a $4.88 trillion valuation, overtaking Nvidia after the chipmaker saw a 3.5% market value drop [6].

items.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Prepares to Shrink $6.7T Balance Sheet; July Rate Decision Likely to Hold Steady
items.summary: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh faces the challenge of shrinking the Fed's $6.7 trillion balance sheet without destabilizing banks, while experts predict the Fed will hold interest rates steady at its July meeting following better-than-expected inflation data [9][10].

items.title: China's Q2 GDP Growth Slows to 4.3%, Benchmark Lending Rates Expected Unchanged
items.summary: China's economy grew 4.3% in Q2, the weakest pace since 2022, and is set to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged for a 14th consecutive month despite softer economic data highlighting uneven growth [2][9].

items.title: SK Hynix Plunges 11.5% as Chip Sector Volatility Continues
items.summary: SK Hynix Inc. dropped 11.53% in the past 24 hours, reflecting continued volatility in the chip sector following last week's pullback [5].

items.title: DAX Edges Down 0.34% While Oil Indices Surge Over 2%
items.summary: The DAX fell 0.34% on Monday, while the S&P GSCI Crude Oil Index rose 2.35% and the Petroleum Index gained 2.24% amid Middle East tensions [5].

items.title: US to Impose 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Amid Trade Tensions
items.summary: The U.S. announced new 25% tariffs on some Brazilian imports over alleged unfair practices, though beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials escaped the tariffs [6].

items.title: UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham Takes Office After Starmer Resignation
items.summary: Andy Burnham became the UK's fifth prime minister in four years after receiving the request to form a government from King Charles, following Labour leader Keir Starmer's resignation [10].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Stay Flat Near $64,700 and $1,872 Amid U.S.–Iran Tensions
items.summary: Major cryptocurrencies moved sideways as U.S. airstrikes on Iran continued for nine nights, with Bitcoin hovering between $64,000–$65,000 and Ethereum stuck near $1,800 despite a 12% surge in trading volume[1][13].

items.title: Iran Ceasefire Proposal Reverses Risk-Off Move: Bitcoin Rises 1% to $64,900
items.summary: A Reuters report that Iran proposed a 10-day ceasefire reversed Monday's sell-off, lifting Bitcoin ~1% to $64,900 while oil prices fell ~$3/barrel to $80[2].

items.title: Crypto Market Slips as Bears Drive Price Action; $85.99M in Liquidations, Shorts Dominate
items.summary: The crypto market drifted lower with Bitcoin down ~1% since midnight UTC and negative 24-hour cumulative volume delta across major coins; $85.99M in liquidations occurred, with shorts accounting for $60.01M[5][10].

items.title: Meme Sector Resilient: PEPE +4.38%, BUILDon +31.65%, Pump.fun +22.76%
items.summary: While broader crypto sectors pulled back, the meme sector rose 0.56% in 24 hours, led by PEPE, BUILDon, and Pump.fun, even as Bitcoin dipped 0.25% and ETH edged up 0.52%[12].

items.title: Lorenzo Protocol (BANK) Surges 99% to $0.2277 Amid Sideways Market
items.summary: Lorenzo Protocol's BANK token jumped 99.05% to $0.2277, the standout altcoin mover while top-tier tokens traded flat[1].

items.title: Russia's Parliament Set for Final Crypto Bill Readings Tuesday; Rules for Investors and Cross-Border Payments Included
items.summary: Russia's State Duma will consider the crypto regulation bill in second and third readings Tuesday, incorporating investor rules and cross-border payment provisions[4][8].

items.title: U.S. Agencies Miss GENIUS Act Deadline for Final Stablecoin Rules; Tether Faces 2028 Compliance Deadline
items.summary: U.S. regulators missed the GENIUS Act deadline for final stablecoin rules, stalling progress; offshore issuers like Tether may need compliance by July 2028 to avoid U.S. exchange delisting[8][11][15].

items.title: Pi Network Plans V25 Protocol Upgrade on July 22 with Zero-Knowledge and Privacy Smart Contracts
items.summary: Pi Network will upgrade to V25 on July 22, introducing BN254 cryptography, Poseidon hashing, zero-knowledge proofs, and privacy-focused smart contracts, first on testnet before mainnet[9].

items.title: Cardano Activates Van Rossem Hard Fork; Zcash Releases Faster Zakura Node Ahead of July 28 Upgrade
items.summary: Cardano activated Protocol Version 11 (Van Rossem) via on-chain governance, lowering smart contract costs and adding cryptographic features; Zcash released Zakura 1.0.0 with faster sync and data pruning, supporting the July 28 Ironwood upgrade[8][15].

items.title: Kraken Launches Dollar-Settled Bitcoin and Ethereum European Options on Kraken Pro
items.summary: Kraken introduced dollar-settled European options for BTC and ETH on Kraken Pro, targeting qualified international clients before global expansion[15].

items.title: XRP Open Interest Hits $2.60B, Overtaking HYPE Amid Renewed Institutional Interest
items.summary: XRP's open interest reached $2.60B, surpassing HYPE, as institutional interest in XRP renewed[14].

items.title: FTX Sets $900M Creditor Payout for July 31; Fifth Round in Bankruptcy Recovery
items.summary: FTX will distribute nearly $900M to eligible creditors on July 31, marking its fifth payout round in the bankruptcy recovery process[11].

items.title: France Blocks Access to Polymarket; Vietnam Sets $1,900 Fine for Unlicensed Crypto Trading
items.summary: France blocked access to the crypto prediction market platform Polymarket, while Vietnam introduced $1,900 fines for unlicensed crypto trading[14][11].

items.title: Ansem's Solana Meme Coin Holdings Drop 60% to ~$98M in Under Two Weeks
items.summary: Arkham data shows trader Ansem's Solana meme coin holdings fell from a $260M peak to about $98M in under two weeks, a 60% drop[15].

items.title: Binance and Bybit Saw $2.3B in Combined Stablecoin Outflows Over Past 30 Days
items.summary: Binance and Bybit experienced more than $2.3B in combined stablecoin outflows over the past 30 days, including ~$1.55B from Binance and ~$786M from Bybit[9].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model at 2.8 Trillion Parameters
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a 2.8T-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with a 1M-token context window, claiming frontier-adjacent performance rivaling Anthropic's Fable; the company temporarily paused new subscriptions due to overwhelming demand straining capacity.

items.title: Alibaba Previews Qwen3.8-Max-Preview, Claiming It's Second Only to Anthropic's Fable 5
items.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team released a preview of Qwen3.8-Max, a 2.4T-parameter multimodal MoE model it calls the second-best AI system globally after Fable 5, now available at 10% of standard pricing on Token Plan and Qoder platforms.

items.title: NVIDIA Ships Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B-Parameter On-Device World Model for Robotics
items.summary: NVIDIA introduced Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B-parameter Nemotron-based world model running on-device for vision reasoning and robot policy generation across Jetson, RTX GPUs, and DGX systems, with 22 Japanese robotics giants signing on for adoption.

items.title: Apple Accuses OpenAI of Stealing Trade Secrets in Bid to Build ChatGPT Hardware
items.summary: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging misconduct to extract confidential information from current and former Apple employees as the company seeks to develop its own hardware for ChatGPT integration.

items.title: Trump Administration Shifts Power by Dictating Access to Frontier AI Models
items.summary: Sources report the Trump administration is taking steps to control who accesses the latest frontier AI models, shifting power away from tech giants and potentially reviving efforts to ban foreign open-source AI models, including Chinese releases like Kimi K3.

items.title: New York Imposes One-Year Moratorium on Large AI Data Centers to Protect Energy Grid
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers fueling artificial intelligence for up to a year, becoming the first U.S. state to impose such a moratorium to protect the environment and energy grid amid growing backlash against AI infrastructure expansion.

items.title: Mithra AI Launches Platform to Verify Enterprise Data Before Models See It
items.summary: ShelterZoom subsidiary Mithra Technologies launched Mithra AI on July 20, 2026, a platform designed to verify enterprise data quality and integrity before it reaches AI models, addressing critical data vetting needs in enterprise AI deployment.

items.title: Meta Pulls Instagram AI Feature After Criticism Over AI-Generated Image Abuse
items.summary: Meta disabled a recently launched AI tool feature on Instagram following criticism that the feature allowed accounts to be used as fodder for creating AI-generated images, raising concerns about unauthorized content generation.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bethesda Teases Fallout 5 Following Xbox Mass Layoffs
items.summary: Bethesda has hinted at the upcoming release of Fallout 5 shortly after Xbox announced significant layoffs, while also revealing a roadmap that includes four Fallout games, Elder Scrolls 6, and Creation Engine 3.

items.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved Confirmed for PS5 Release on July 28
items.summary: Master Chief is set to appear on PlayStation 5 as the survival sandbox game Halo: Campaign Evolved launches on July 28, 2026, marking a major cross-platform release.

items.title: Sony Faces Backlash Over Plan to Eliminate Physical Game Discs
items.summary: PlayStation uploaded new trailers for Marvel's Wolverine and Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls, but user comments are dominated by outrage over the company's rumored plan to end support for physical game discs.

items.title: EU Expected to Approve Saudi PIF's $55 Billion EA Acquisition
items.summary: The European Union is reportedly preparing to grant final approval for the Public Investment Fund of Saudi Arabia's historic $55 billion takeover of Electronic Arts.

items.title: UK Game Store GAME Falls into Administration with £16M Debt
items.summary: Major UK video game retailer GAME has entered administration due to £16 million in debt, signaling further instability in the physical retail sector.

items.title: Palworld Surges to Top 15 Steam All-Time Chart After 1.0 Launch
items.summary: Palworld has reclaimed Steam's top concurrency charts with over 921,000 concurrent players following its version 1.0 launch, marking a significant resurgence for the survival title.

items.title: Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls Beta Opens Ahead of July Release
items.summary: PlayStation, Marvel, and Arc System Works are hosting an open beta this week for the upcoming fighting game Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls, which launches on PS5 and PC in less than three weeks.

items.title: Call of Duty Movie Confirmed for Modern Warfare Timeline
items.summary: The upcoming Call of Duty film will be set within Infinity Ward's Modern Warfare timeline, expanding the franchise's cinematic universe.

items.title: EA FC 27 Release Date Set for September 25 with Early Access
items.summary: EA has announced that EA FC 27 will be released globally on September 25, 2026, with Early Access beginning as early as September 18.

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced Hits 3 Million Sales
items.summary: The remastered version of Assassin's Creed: Black Flag has surpassed three million copies sold worldwide, validating the director's bet on year-long support.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SpaceX Targets July 23 for Next Starship Launch After Last-Moment Pad Abort
items.summary: SpaceX announced Sunday it is targeting Thursday, July 23, for another Starship rocket launch attempt following a last-second abort on the pad that delayed the previous test flight until at least next week.

items.title: TSMC Pledges Additional $100 Billion to Expand US Chipmaking Capacity in Arizona
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) confirmed plans to invest another $100 billion in its Arizona facilities, bringing its total US commitment to $265 billion despite facing construction worker shortages.

items.title: Alibaba's AliExpress Hit with Record €550 Million EU Fine for Counterfeit Sales
items.summary: The European Union fined Alibaba's AliExpress platform €550 million ($629 million) on Monday for failing to prevent the sale of illegal, unsafe, and counterfeit products.

items.title: ePlane Unveils Full-Scale e200X Electric Aircraft in Chennai, Targets 2027 Certification
items.summary: Indian startup ePlane unveiled its full-scale e200X electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft in Chennai, aiming to begin certified flight tests by mid-2027.

items.title: WhatsApp Will Soon Allow Users to Use Username Instead of Phone Number
items.summary: WhatsApp announced Monday that users will soon have the option to use usernames instead of phone numbers to address a long-standing privacy vulnerability.

items.title: Apple Files Lawsuit Against OpenAI Alleging Trade Secret Theft for Hardware Plans
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit on Friday accusing OpenAI of stealing trade secrets as the company seeks to build its own hardware to support ChatGPT integration.

items.title: EU Forces Google to Share Search Data and Open Android to Rival AI Companies
items.summary: The European Union issued new rules requiring Google to share search data and open its Android operating system to rival AI companies, though this regulation specifically targets AI competition.

items.title: Amazon to Launch Satellite Internet Service in South Africa
items.summary: Amazon is set to launch its satellite internet service in South Africa, potentially beating Elon Musk's SpaceX Starlink in Musk's home country.

items.title: Meta Faces Tennessee Trial Over Instagram's Design and Youth Mental Health Crisis
items.summary: Meta Platforms began a trial in Tennessee on Monday over state claims that Instagram's design intentionally contributed to a youth mental health crisis by making the platform addictive.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large Data Centers for One Year Amid Energy and Climate Concerns
items.summary: New York state announced it will block the construction of new large data centers for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, though this decision is tied to AI infrastructure needs.
